text.title: Claude Code Hooks Reference Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the hooks methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Hook MUST declare both `event` and `matcher` (or `matcher: "*"` for global). Implicit matching is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Explicit matchers let the user and reviewers see what the hook applies to without reading the script body.

rule.statement: Hook script MUST complete within 60s (Claude Code default). Long-running logic MUST be moved to an Agent or external job.
rule.rationale: Hooks block tool execution; runtime > 60s degrades the agent experience.

rule.statement: Hook side effects MUST be reversible (formatting, logging, context injection). Irreversible operations (deploys, billing, deletes) MUST require a confirmation dialog OR run from a Command instead.
rule.rationale: Hooks run before confirmation dialogs; irreversible actions in hooks bypass user intent.

rule.statement: PreToolUse hooks MUST exit 0 to allow tool, exit 2 to block, exit 1 for transient errors (retry). Mixing semantics is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Exit codes are the only channel hooks have to communicate; muddy codes cause silent blocks or unwanted retries.

rule.statement: Every Bash-blocking PreToolUse hook MUST log to a structured audit file (JSONL) with timestamp, tool, args_hash, decision, reason.
rule.rationale: Without audit log, blocked tool calls become invisible and devs lose trust in the hook system.
